hero.question: Will Scottie Scheffler win the 2026 Masters tournament?
hero.prob_take:
At 24.5%, the market is pricing Scheffler as a 1-in-4 favorite two years out, which feels about right for the best golfer on the planet when regression to the mean and injury risk are baked in.

The +11 point surge suggests fresh money chasing recent form, but that's exactly when you fade a hyped name, Scheffler's dominance invites overconfidence, and major championship variance is real enough to keep this fair or even slightly overpriced.

daily_take.headline: Scheffler's Masters odds just jumped 11 points. The market is overreacting.
daily_take.deck: Scottie Scheffler's 2026 Masters probability spiked to 24.5% on $2.3M volume, likely on the back of his recent form. But here's the sharp angle: golf odds are notoriously volatile and tournament-specific. One hot week doesn't change his structural edge at Augusta. The smart play is fading the recency bias. Back him at higher odds later, or skip the overheated position entirely.

sidebar.headline: Iran ceasefire odds collapsed 12 points. Trump's rhetoric has the market spooked, but geopolitical prices swing on headlines. Wait for clarity before committing capital.
sidebar.label: Trump Iran ceasefire: 36.5%

sidebar.headline: McIlroy's Masters odds just spiked 14.5 points on Scheffler momentum. Two elite golfers, one tournament. The math doesn't work. One of these is wrong.
sidebar.label: Rory McIlroy: 21.0%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz traffic odds barely moved on escalating tensions. Either the market priced this in weeks ago, or shipping risk is cheaper than it should be.
sidebar.label: Hormuz normal traffic: 26.5%
